source: Hand-curated facts from MLB.com, Baseball Hall of Fame, Baseball Reference, and standard baseball records
note: 30-question seed. Daily pick is deterministic by day-of-year (same question for same date). Rotate by adding to this file — no schema change needed. All answers are verifiable public records.

questions.question: Who holds the MLB single-season home run record?
questions.answer: Barry Bonds — 73 in 2001 (San Francisco Giants).

questions.question: How many World Series titles have the St. Louis Cardinals won?
Who was the last player to hit .400 in a season?
questions.answer: Ted Williams — .406 in 1941 (Boston Red Sox). No one has done it since.

questions.question: What team did Jackie Robinson debut with when he broke the MLB color barrier?
questions.answer: Brooklyn Dodgers — April 15, 1947, at Ebbets Field against the Boston Braves.

questions.question: Who holds the record for most career stolen bases?
questions.answer: Rickey Henderson — 1,406 career steals. Lou Brock is second with 938.

questions.question: Which player's consecutive-games-played streak stood at 2,130 from 1939 to 1995?
questions.answer: Lou Gehrig. Cal Ripken Jr. passed it on September 6, 1995, finishing at 2,632.

questions.question: Who is the only player to win an MVP award in both the American and National Leagues?
questions.answer: Frank Robinson — NL MVP with Cincinnati in 1961, AL MVP with Baltimore in 1966.

questions.question: How many consecutive games did Joe DiMaggio hit safely in 1941?
questions.answer: 56. The streak ran May 15 through July 16, 1941 and remains MLB's longest.

questions.question: Which MLB franchise was originally known as the Brown Stockings, founded in 1882?
questions.answer: The St. Louis Cardinals. They joined the NL in 1892 and were renamed Cardinals in 1900.

questions.question: Who was the first Black player in the American League?
questions.answer: Larry Doby, July 5, 1947, with the Cleveland Indians — 11 weeks after Jackie Robinson.

questions.question: What is the only World Series perfect game?
questions.answer: Don Larsen, October 8, 1956 — Yankees vs Brooklyn Dodgers, Game 5.

questions.question: Who threw the last 30-win season in the National League?
questions.answer: Dizzy Dean — 30-7 for the 1934 Cardinals Gas House Gang.

questions.question: Which pitcher posted a 1.12 ERA in 1968, the lowest in live-ball era history?
questions.answer: Bob Gibson. His dominance contributed to MLB lowering the pitcher's mound for 1969.

questions.question: Who is the all-time MLB home run leader?
questions.answer: Barry Bonds — 762 career home runs.

questions.question: What year was the first MLB night game played?
questions.answer: 1935. Cincinnati hosted Philadelphia at Crosley Field under newly installed lights on May 24.

questions.question: How many times have the Washington Nationals won the World Series?
questions.answer: Once, in 2019 — their first since relocating from Montreal in 2005.

questions.question: Who was the only player unanimously elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame on the first ballot?
questions.answer: Mariano Rivera, 2019 — 425 of 425 votes.

questions.question: Which MLB team has the longest championship drought broken since 2000?
questions.answer: The Chicago Cubs — 108 years, from 1908 to 2016.

questions.question: Who was the first Latin American player elected to the Hall of Fame?
questions.answer: Roberto Clemente, 1973 — the five-year waiting period was waived after his death in a relief mission to Nicaragua.

questions.question: Which pitcher has the most career strikeouts in MLB history?
questions.answer: Nolan Ryan — 5,714 career strikeouts.

questions.question: How many back-to-back no-hitters have been thrown in MLB history?
questions.answer: One. Johnny Vander Meer of the Reds threw consecutive no-hitters on June 11 and June 15, 1938.

questions.question: Who was the first MLB player to enlist after Pearl Harbor?
questions.answer: Bob Feller, the day after the attack. He served four years on the USS Alabama as a gun captain.

questions.question: What franchise lineage leads to today's Minnesota Twins?
questions.answer: The original Washington Senators (1901-1960). Relocated to Bloomington, MN and renamed Twins in 1961.

questions.question: Who was the first Black umpire in MLB?
questions.answer: Emmett Ashford — first game April 11, 1966 (Cleveland vs Washington). He was 51 years old.

questions.question: Which Cardinals Hall of Famer was traded from the Cubs for pitcher Ernie Broglio in 1964?
questions.answer: Lou Brock. The deal is considered one of the most lopsided trades in MLB history.

questions.question: What was the nickname of the Depression-era Cardinals championship team?
questions.answer: The Gas House Gang. They won the 1934 World Series, led by the Dean brothers.

questions.question: How many MLB teams existed when Jackie Robinson debuted in 1947?
questions.answer: 16 — eight in each league. Expansion began in 1961.

questions.question: Which franchise moved from Montreal to Washington in 2005?
questions.answer: The Expos became the Washington Nationals. The original Senators (1901-1960) and the expansion Senators (1961-1971) were different franchises.

questions.question: Who hit a walk-off home run to win the 1960 World Series?
questions.answer: Bill Mazeroski of the Pittsburgh Pirates — the only walk-off home run in a World Series Game 7.

questions.question: Which Cardinals manager is known for the style called 'Whiteyball'?
questions.answer: Whitey Herzog, managed 1980-1990. Style emphasized speed, defense, and pitching over home runs.
